agreements.title: Necrotizing Fasciitis Service Agreement
summary_bullets: NSTI is a life-threatening emergency; delayed debridement increases mortality.
If NSTI is clinically obvious, do not delay operative escalation for scoring or additional diagnostics.
Start immediate resuscitation, broad-spectrum IV antibiotics, analgesia, and OR preparation.
If stable and diagnosis is uncertain, perform minimum ED workup: risk factors, full vital signs, and LRINEC-related labs.
LRINEC >= 6 is strongly concerning; LRINEC < 5 is only reassuring when exam and imaging are also reassuring.
LRINEC cannot be used alone to rule out NSTI.
In stable patients, ultrasound can be used early but has higher false-negative risk than CT.
CT with IV contrast showing soft-tissue gas should prompt immediate surgical intervention; fascial edema/fluid collections also increase concern.
Upload a clinical photo to the chart when feasible to support consultant assessment and serial comparison.
Primary consult is General Surgery after initial workup, with location-based subspecialty involvement and post-stabilization ownership transitions.
ed_actions: Do not delay operative escalation when exam is clearly consistent with NSTI.
Initiate IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, analgesia, and sepsis-focused resuscitation immediately.
If diagnosis is uncertain and the patient is stable, order CBC, BMP, CRP, glucose, creatinine, hemoglobin, sodium, and calculate LRINEC.
Obtain CT with IV contrast in stable patients when diagnosis remains uncertain after initial assessment.
Upload a clinical photo of the affected extremity in the chart when possible.
Consult General Surgery early regardless of anatomic location once initial workup is complete.
consults_and_escalation: Primary consult: General Surgery.
Below elbow involvement: Hand Surgery assists and can assume primary floor management after stabilization.
Below knee involvement: Orthopedics assists and can assume primary floor management after stabilization.
Above elbow or knee involvement: General Surgery remains primary.
When infection crosses elbow/knee boundaries, services co-manage based on predominant involvement and urgency.
Consult Plastic Surgery for graft/flap coverage after stabilization and wound control when needed.
disposition_or_ownership: General Surgery is primary during ICU-level management after operative source control.
Post-stabilization ownership transitions by anatomic location per the service agreement.
If NSTI is excluded, revert to non-necrotizing limb-infection consultation pathways by location.
timing_targets: Timing expectation: Immediate surgical consultation and debridement when NSTI is clinically apparent.
agreements.redaction_notes: Potential contact details or identifiers were intentionally excluded from published fields.

Decision points: Score of 7 or higher is highly suspicious for appendicitis.
Decision points: Score below 5 is unlikely to be appendicitis.

Engage the designated specialty once inclusion criteria are met.

agreements.title: Urology and Emergency Medicine Service Agreement 2019
agreements.source_filename: Service agreements/Urology and Emergency Medicine Service Agreement 2019.docx
summary_bullets: Urgent/emergent urologic conditions requiring immediate Urology call include torsion, persistent priapism, penile fracture, unreducible paraphimosis, and refractory catheter/hematuria scenarios.
Fournier's gangrene of the scrotum or penis requires immediate specialist escalation; if perirectal involvement is present or unclear, involve General Surgery.
Infected or high-risk obstruction (obstructing stone with fever >38C, solitary-kidney obstruction, bilateral obstruction) is treated as time-sensitive and requires immediate Urology involvement.

Hospital Medicine admission can proceed without overnight Urology call (7 PM-7 AM) for select non-fever obstructive pain/vomiting, controlled hematuria on CBI, or septic epididymo-orchitis without abscess/gangrene.
Stable patients with larger stones, urinary retention after Foley, hydronephrosis, or renal/testicular masses are directed to outpatient Urology referral without ED Urology consult.
Small uncomplicated stones, non-obstructive UTI/pyelo, balanitis/posthitis, uncomplicated epididymo-orchitis, benign cystic findings, and negative-ultrasound testicular pain follow PCP pathway.
Acute urinary retention after Foley placement should include tamsulosin initiation and outpatient Urology planning when otherwise stable.
ed_actions: Call Urology immediately for torsion, persistent priapism, penile fracture, unreducible paraphimosis, infected obstruction, refractory hematuria, or difficult catheterization with acute retention.
For Fournier's gangrene, call Urology immediately and add General Surgery if perirectal extension is present or uncertain.
If hematuria persists despite CBI and repeated Foley irrigation, treat as urgent and escalate to Urology.
For stable urinary retention after Foley placement, start tamsulosin and arrange outpatient Urology referral.
Document pathway category (urgent call, admit/no overnight call, outpatient referral, or PCP follow-up) in the ED note.
consults_and_escalation: Immediate consult pathway: Urology for all urgent/emergent categories.
Cross-service escalation: Add General Surgery for suspected Fournier's with perirectal involvement or unclear anatomic extension.
No overnight Urology call window for specified admit category: 7 PM-7 AM.
Escalate atypical high-risk presentations that do not cleanly fit categories via attending-to-attending discussion.
disposition_or_ownership: Admit to Hospital Medicine (without overnight Urology call, 7 PM-7 AM) for non-fever obstructive pain/vomiting, controlled gross hematuria on CBI, or septic epididymo-orchitis without abscess/gangrene.

Outpatient Urology referral (no ED consult) for stable obstruction >3 mm without fever, asymptomatic hydronephrosis, renal stone >=10 mm, post-Foley retention, asymptomatic renal mass/complex cyst, or testicular mass.

PCP follow-up (no Urology referral) for obstruction <=3 mm without fever, non-obstructive UTI/pyelo, balanitis/posthitis, uncomplicated epididymo-orchitis, renal stone <10 mm without obstruction, benign hydrocele/epididymal cyst, negative-ultrasound testicular pain, or microlithiasis.
timing_targets: Timing expectation: Immediate Urology contact for urgent/emergent conditions.
Timing expectation: No overnight Urology call required from 7 PM-7 AM for defined Hospital Medicine admit scenarios.
agreements.redaction_notes: No explicit sensitive fields were carried into output; summaries are intentionally high-level.

agreements.title: Kaiser Afib Protocol
summary_bullets: Decision points: If CHA2DS2VASc score ≥ 1, consider warfarin.
Key rule: Arrange outpatient echocardiography if first episode.
Practical note: Consult cardiology for possible pharmacologic vs electrical cardioversion if indicated.
Practical note: If Heart Rate > 100-110 bpm.
Practical note: Metoprolol IV (5 mg doses every 15 min); convert to oral Metoprolol 25-50 mg BID if heart rate < 100 bpm.
Practical note: If onset is clear and < 48 hr, Pharmacologic or electrical cardioversion is safe.
Practical note: Home within 1 hour after receiving oral medication and HR remains controlled (<110).
ed_actions: Stabilize immediate threats and document objective severity findings before handoff.
Initiate pathway-specific treatment and diagnostics before consultant transfer of ownership.
Record all consultant communication and explicit ownership in the chart.
Complete sepsis bundle steps and reassess perfusion while disposition is being arranged.
consults_and_escalation: Consult flow: Consult cardiology for possible pharmacologic vs electrical cardioversion if indicated.
Engage the designated specialty once inclusion criteria are met.
Escalate to attending/chief pathways when callback is delayed or recommendations conflict.
Document closed-loop communication for each escalation step.
disposition_or_ownership: Ownership/disposition: Arrange outpatient echocardiography if first episode.
Ownership/disposition: Discharge home on diltiazem (or metoprolol).
ED retains active ownership until receiving service acceptance is explicitly documented.
Receiving service assumes ongoing plan after acceptance criteria are satisfied.
If status changes before transfer/admission, disposition should be re-evaluated under ED oversight.

Timing expectation: Metoprolol IV (5 mg doses every 15 min); convert to oral Metoprolol 25-50 mg BID if heart rate < 100.
Timing expectation: If onset is clear and < 48 hr, Pharmacologic or electrical cardioversion is safe.
Timing expectation: Home within 1 hour after receiving oral medication and HR remains controlled (<110).

agreements.title: Prolonged ED Length of Stay Service Line Agreement
summary_bullets: Non-admitted patients in the ED for 24 or more hours with no medical indication for admission but disposition barriers will be evaluated for hospital medicine consult and OBS admission.
Agreement between Emergency Medicine, Hospital Medicine, Care Without Delay, AAMD, and Area Medical Director at Ontario and Fontana medical centers.
Applies to members and non-members. Excludes admitted patients awaiting bed, psychiatric patients without medical need, and pediatric patients.
All patients with ED LOS 24 or more hours and no discharge plan must have case management and social services consults at minimum.
If the multidisciplinary team agrees no disposition is secured within 12 hours of the 24-hour threshold, CWD physician reviews; if criteria are met, ED physician requests hospital medicine consult.
Hospital medicine accepts as primary team under observation status for physician continuity, improved family communication, and MDT coordination.
ED physician signs off but remains available for acute decompensation including airway emergencies and code blue.
Hospital medicine consultation will be requested and completed during daytime hours, 7 days a week.
AAMD and AMD notified during daytime hours when patient is admitted for disposition barriers.
ed_actions: Evaluate all ED patients for medical need for admission versus disposition barriers.
Escalate to multidisciplinary team (social worker, case manager, CWD physician, geriatrician, hospice liaison) as early as possible when indicated.
Ensure case management and social services consults are placed for any patient with ED LOS 24 or more hours and no discharge plan.
Discuss qualifying patients in morning Care Without Delay huddle daily.
Request hospital medicine consult when CWD physician agrees patient meets PLOS criteria.
Sign off after hospital medicine consult but remain available for acute decompensation.
Notify AAMD and AMD during daytime hours of patient being admitted for disposition barriers.
consults_and_escalation: Case management and social services consult required for all patients with ED LOS 24 or more hours and no discharge plan.
CWD physician must be discussed with if no disposition is secured within 12 hours of the 24-hour threshold.
If CWD physician agrees criteria are met, ED physician requests hospital medicine consult.
On weekends or when no local CWD coverage is available, ED case manager will discuss with CWD on-call physician.
Hospital medicine consultation completed during daytime hours, 7 days a week. Avoid after-hours consultation unless medically indicated.
disposition_or_ownership: Hospital medicine accepts as primary team and admits to observation status after consultation.
ED physician signs off after hospital medicine consult but remains available for acute medical decompensation.
If patient decompensates and meets admission criteria at any time, follow standard ED-to-hospital medicine consult workflow.
AAMD and AMD must be notified during daytime hours when patient is admitted for disposition barriers.
timing_targets: Trigger: ED LOS reaches 24 hours with no discharge plan
Daily CWD huddle discussion for all qualifying patients
Hospital medicine consult if no disposition within 12 hours of 24-hour threshold
Hospital medicine consultation: daytime hours only, 7 days a week
agreements.redaction_notes: Signatory names retained for reference; verify against current signed source document.

agreements.title: Non-member Transfer Process
summary_bullets: Secure Chat Alerts: ED physicians will receive notifications for patients requiring authorization.
One-Hour Rule: Health plan must respond within 1 hour; if no response, authorization defaults to admit.
Transfer Timeframe: Maximum 2–3 hours wait for a bed at an outside facility; if no bed, admit locally.
Scope: Managed Care Commercial, Managed Medicare, Managed Medi-Cal.
Go-Live: April 13
Pilot Duration: 4–6 weeks with weekly check-ins.
ed_actions: Monitor Secure Chat for authorization notifications on non-member patients.
If no health plan response within 1 hour, authorization defaults to admit — proceed accordingly.
Wait up to 2–3 hours for an outside bed; if no bed available, admit locally.
consults_and_escalation: Health plan must respond to authorization requests within 1 hour.
If no response within 1 hour, authorization is assumed and patient may be admitted.
disposition_or_ownership: If outside bed secured within 2–3 hours, transfer per standard process.
If no outside bed within 2–3 hours, admit locally regardless of member status.
timing_targets: Health plan response: within 1 hour of authorization request
Outside bed wait: maximum 2–3 hours before defaulting to local admit
